title: presets

messages.description: sets a preset number and loads it
save preset (same as 'save' without a float)
presets as a list when receiving "export" message

flags.name: -file <symbol>
flags.description:
set a file to import from
default: none

flags.name: -morph <f, f>
flags.description:
sets morph time and grain
default: 0 20

flags.name: -interp
flags.description:
sets to interpolation mode
default: no interpolation

flags.name: -exp <float>
flags.description:
sets exponential factor for morph/interpolation
default: 1

methods.description: load from a preset number. If no float is given, the last set preset number is loaded
save to a preset number. If no float is given, the last set preset is saved
sets morph time and grain for morphing between presets
clear data from preset number, no float clears all presets
open the preset window so you can see/edit values
import presets from a file, no symbol opens dialog window
write presets to file, no symbol opens dialog window

body: [presets] manages presets for patches and abstractions and has interpolation and morphing features. Change the number and symbol below, save a preset and the patch, reopen to recall the presets. Use the "read" message to restore to this example's default.
